metadata.title: Renaissance DM Tool - Utilities and Logic Specification
metadata.description: Comprehensive specification of all utility functions, service classes, parsers, comparators, and storage logic. Detailed enough to reimplement every function with identical behavior.

cleanName.description: Strips quotes, backticks, and square brackets from identifier names, then trims whitespace
algorithm: 1. If str is falsy, return empty string
2. Replace all occurrences of double-quote, backtick, open bracket, close bracket with empty string

splitSqlParams.description: Splits a SQL parameter list by commas, respecting nested parentheses (e.g. does not split inside NUMERIC(10,2))
algorithm: 1. Initialize empty result array, empty buffer string, depth counter = 0
2. Iterate over each character in txt:
a. If char is '(', increment depth
b. If char is ')', decrement depth
c. If char is ',' AND depth == 0: push buffer to result, reset buffer to empty
d. Otherwise: append char to buffer
3. If buffer has non-whitespace content, push to result

normalizeType.description: Normalizes PostgreSQL type aliases to their canonical form
algorithm: 1. Trim, convert to uppercase, collapse all whitespace to nothing

parsePostgreSQL.description: Parses PostgreSQL DDL script into structured ScriptData with tables, columns, and constraints
algorithm: - Uses paren-depth matching to find the body between opening and closing parentheses
- Body is passed to splitSqlParams() to split into lines
3. FOR EACH LINE IN TABLE BODY:
a. INLINE PRIMARY KEY (line starts with CONSTRAINT or PRIMARY KEY, and contains PRIMARY KEY):
- Extract column list, clean with cleanName(), join with ', '
- Add constraint and track pkCols set
b. INLINE UNIQUE (line starts with CONSTRAINT or UNIQUE, and contains UNIQUE):
c. INLINE FOREIGN KEY (line starts with CONSTRAINT or FOREIGN, and contains FOREIGN KEY):
- Extract constraint name, local cols, ref schema (default: 'public'), ref table, ref cols
- Guard: line must NOT start with CONSTRAINT, PRIMARY, FOREIGN, UNIQUE, or CHECK
- If match, preserve full clause as default
- Else if rest contains 'GENERATED', use 'GENERATED AS IDENTITY'
- Clean: remove PostgreSQL casts (::type), remove single quotes
- INLINE PK: if rest contains 'PRIMARY KEY', add column name to pkCols
4. POST-TABLE: If pkCols set is non-empty and no PK constraint yet, add PK constraint
- Only add if table doesn't already have a PK constraint

parseOracleSQL.description: Parses Oracle SQL DDL into structured ScriptData
algorithm: 1. COMMENT REMOVAL: Same as PostgreSQL parser
- Uses manual paren-depth matching (handles nested STORAGE(...) etc.)
3. FOR EACH LINE IN TABLE BODY:
a. CONSTRAINTS: Same as PostgreSQL (PK, UNIQUE) - also handles CHECK keyword to skip
- TYPE WITH ARGS: if rest starts with '(', extract /^\s*\(([^)]+)\)/
- Note: Oracle-specific ENABLE/DISABLE keywords added to stop-pattern
Extended regex handles Oracle clauses: ENABLE, DISABLE, NOT DEFERRABLE, DEFERRABLE, INITIALLY IMMEDIATE/DEFERRED, VALIDATE, NOVALIDATE, ON DELETE CASCADE/SET NULL
5. PARSE ALTER TABLE PRIMARY KEYS: Same pattern as FK but for PRIMARY KEY
- Only UNIQUE indexes are added as constraints (type: 'Unique')
8. PARSE ALTER TABLE MODIFY NOT NULL:

normalizeOracleType.description: Maps Oracle types to canonical uppercase forms. Currently maps to self (identity mapping for known types), returns type.toUpperCase() for all inputs

parseDBML.description: Parses DBML (Database Markup Language) into structured ScriptData
algorithm: 3. FOR EACH LINE IN TABLE CONTENT:
- Skip empty lines, lines starting with 'Note:', 'indexes', '{', '}'
- Group 2: type (with optional precision, spaces collapsed)
a. nullable: settings contains 'not null' -> 'No', else 'Yes'
c. pk: settings contains 'pk' or 'primary key' -> add to pkCols set
d. unique: settings contains 'unique' -> add to uniqueCols set
- Type is normalized with normalizeType() (spaces collapsed first)
4. ADD CONSTRAINTS from accumulated pkCols, uniqueCols, fkCols
- Matches patterns like: Ref: schema.table.col < schema.table.col
- Finds source table and adds FK constraint with ref format: '{tgtSchema}.{tgtTable}({tgtCol})'

parsePUML.description: Parses PlantUML activity diagram syntax into a structured PUMLDiagram with nodes, connections, swimlanes, partitions, and notes
parsePUML.returnType: PUMLDiagram = { name, nodes, connections, swimlanes, partitions, notes }
parsePUML.controlFlowStack: Stack tracks nested if/fork/while/repeat blocks with branches

swimlane.description: Parses |Name| or |#color|Name| syntax. Creates swimlane if not exists, sets currentSwimlane

detach.note: Sets lastNodeId to null (ends current branch)

if.description: Pushes {type:'if', nodeId, branches:[{label:thenLabel, lastNodeId:null}]} to controlStack

elseif.description: Saves current branch ending, creates new decision node, connects with 'no' condition from previous decision

else.description: Saves current branch ending, adds new branch, resets lastNodeId to decision node

endif.description: Pops controlStack, creates invisible merge node (empty activity), connects all branch ends to merge point

forkAgain.description: Saves current branch ending, adds new branch, resets lastNodeId to fork node

endFork.description: Creates join node, connects all branch ends to join

endwhile.description: Connects lastNodeId back to while condition (loop), creates exit node with exitLabel condition

repeatWhile.description: Creates repeat-end decision node, connects back to repeat-start for loop

floatingNote.description: Creates floating note not attached to any node

arrow.description: Labeled arrows - currently parsed but not fully handled

postProcessing: 1. Update partition nodeIds by iterating all nodes and matching to partitions by name
2. Filter out empty merge nodes (activity nodes with empty text)
3. Update connections to bypass empty merge nodes
4. Return PUMLDiagram with filtered nodes and connections

detectScriptType.description: Auto-detects the SQL dialect of the input content

parseScript.signature: export function parseScript(content: string, type: ScriptType): ScriptData
parseScript.description: Dispatches to the appropriate parser based on script type

reparseScript.signature: export function reparseScript(content: string, type: ScriptType, oldData: ScriptData): ScriptData
reparseScript.description: Re-parses raw content while preserving user-edited column metadata from old data
algorithm: 1. Parse content with parseScript(content, type) to get newData
b. For each new table, look up old table by uppercase name
c. Build oldColMap: column name (UPPERCASE) -> Column
d. Merge: new structural data + old metadata (old values used if non-empty/non-null, using || or ?? operators)
3. Apply mergeMetadata to both targets and sources

quoteIdent.description: Quotes an identifier if it contains special characters
quoteIdent.algorithm: If name matches the simple identifier regex, return as-is. Otherwise wrap in double quotes.

escapeRegex.description: Escapes special regex characters by prefixing with backslash

buildTableNamePattern.signature: function buildTableNamePattern(schema: string, tableName: string): string
buildTableNamePattern.description: Builds a regex pattern to match a table name with optional schema prefix and optional quotes

findCreateTableBlock.signature: function findCreateTableBlock(rawContent: string, table: Table, scriptType: ScriptType): { start: number; end: number } | null
findCreateTableBlock.description: Finds the CREATE TABLE block boundaries. For DBML uses /Table\s+{pattern}\s*(?:as\s+\w+\s*)?\{[\s\S]*?\}/gi. For SQL uses paren-depth matching then skips to semicolon.

findAlterTableFKBlocks.signature: function findAlterTableFKBlocks(rawContent: string, table: Table, scriptType: ScriptType): { start: number; end: number }[]
findAlterTableFKBlocks.description: Finds all ALTER TABLE...FOREIGN KEY blocks for a specific table. Returns sorted in reverse order for safe replacement.

findCreateEnd.signature: function findCreateEnd(text: string, openParenPos: number): number
findCreateEnd.description: Uses paren-depth matching from opening paren to closing paren, then skips to semicolon. Returns index just past semicolon.

extractTableName.description: Extracts table name from CREATE TABLE statement. Returns capture group 2.

findAlterSectionStart.description: Finds the first ALTER TABLE statement NOT inside a CREATE TABLE block range. Returns position or -1.

extractIncomingBlocks.description: Extracts individual CREATE TABLE blocks and ALTER TABLE statements from pasted DDL

generateTableDDL.signature: export function generateTableDDL(table: Table, scriptType: ScriptType): string
generateTableDDL.description: Generates CREATE TABLE DDL + ALTER TABLE FK statements for a single table. Dispatches to generateSQL or generateDBML based on scriptType.

sqlGeneration.inlineConstraints: PK and Unique as inline constraints, FK as separate ALTER TABLE statements

defaultHandling.IDENTITY_value: Reconstructed as 'GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY'

replaceTableDDL.signature: export function replaceTableDDL(rawContent: string, table: Table, newDDL: string, scriptType: ScriptType): string
replaceTableDDL.description: Replaces a specific table's DDL block in the full script. First removes FK ALTER TABLE blocks (reverse order), then replaces CREATE TABLE block. If table not found, appends at end.

generateOmegaHeader.description: Generates a 3-line OMEGA comment header for a table

parseScriptIntoBlocks.description: Decomposes a full OMEGA-format script into preamble, CREATE TABLE blocks, and trailing ALTER TABLE section
parseScriptIntoBlocks.returnType: ParsedScript = { preamble: string, createBlocks: OmegaBlock[], alterSection: string }
algorithm: 1. Find all OMEGA headers with regex
2. If no headers found: find bare CREATE TABLE statements, wrap each with generated OMEGA header
3. If headers found: preamble is content before first header; each header region contains the CREATE TABLE that follows it
4. Find alter section: first ALTER TABLE not inside any CREATE TABLE block

insertOrReplaceMultipleTables.signature: export function insertOrReplaceMultipleTables(rawContent: string, incomingDDL: string): { newContent: string; results: TableInsertResult[] }
insertOrReplaceMultipleTables.description: Inserts or replaces multiple CREATE TABLE statements into an existing OMEGA-format script
algorithm: 3. Build map of existing blocks (keyed by uppercase table name)
5. For each incoming table: replace or insert in blockMap, track action ('replaced' or 'inserted')
6. Sort all blocks A-Z by table name (case-insensitive)
7. Update alter section: remove ALTERs for replaced tables, append incoming ALTERs
8. Reassemble: preamble + sorted blocks + alter section, joined with '\n\n'

types.ParsedScript: { preamble: string; createBlocks: OmegaBlock[]; alterSection: string }

normalizeTypeForCompare.description: Normalizes types for comparison. Same as parser normalizeType but also removes Oracle BYTE/CHAR specifications from size params.
normalizeTypeForCompare.note: FLOAT8 maps to 'DOUBLEPRECISION' (no space) unlike parser which maps to 'DOUBLE PRECISION'

checkTypes.signature: function checkTypes(t1: string, t2: string): TypeCheckResult
checkTypes.returnType: { match: boolean; soft: boolean; reason?: string }
algorithm: 2. If identical: { match: true, soft: false }
- If both start with VARCHAR or VARCHAR2 (any combo), compare bases after removing '2'
- If sizes differ: { match: true, soft: true, reason: 'Size difference' }
- Otherwise: { match: true, soft: true, reason: 'VARCHAR ~ VARCHAR2' }
- If both start with CHAR and neither contains 'VAR'
- Size differences: soft match with 'Size difference'
- Both start with NUMERIC: extract (precision,scale) via getNumericParams()
- Same params: soft match with 'Scale ignored (0)'
- Different params: soft match with 'Precision/scale difference'
- One NUMBER, one NUMERIC: soft match with 'NUMBER ~ NUMERIC'
8. NUMBER with different precision/scale: soft match

getNumericParams.description: Extracts [precision, scale] from a numeric type string. Returns [0, 0] if no match.
getNumericParams.algorithm: Split captured content by comma. First part is precision, second (if exists) is scale.

compareTables.signature: export function compareTables(source: Record<string, Table>, target: Record<string, Table>): Record<string, TableDiff>
compareTables.description: Compares two sets of tables (keyed by uppercase table name) and returns diff results

diffStatuses.MISSING: Table exists in source but not in target
diffStatuses.ADDED: Table exists in target but not in source
diffStatuses.MODIFIED: Hard differences (type mismatches, nullable changes, added/deleted columns)

algorithm: - If in target: run compareColumns() to get diff
2. For each target table not in source: status = 'ADDED'

compareColumns.signature: function compareColumns(src: Table, tgt: Table): { hasHardDiff, hasSoftDiff, pkDiff, changes: ColumnDiff[] }
compareColumns.description: Detailed column-by-column comparison between two tables
algorithm: 1. Compare PK constraints: split localCols by comma, sort, compare joined strings. pkDiff is true if different. pkDiff causes hasHardDiff.
2. Build column maps keyed by uppercase column name for both source and target
3. Get union of all column names
a. Not in source: type='ADDED', hasHardDiff=true
b. Not in target: type='DELETED', hasHardDiff=true
- If nullable changed: type='MODIFIED', hasHardDiff=true, note='Nullable changed'
- If types match soft: type='SOFT', hasSoftDiff=true
- If types don't match: type='MODIFIED', hasHardDiff=true

tablesToMap.signature: export function tablesToMap(tables: Table[]): Record<string, Table>
tablesToMap.description: Converts an array of Tables to a map keyed by uppercase tableName

validateColumnMapping.signature: export function validateColumnMapping(sourceCol: Column, targetCol: Column, sourceTable: Table, targetTable: Table, ruleSets: TypeRuleSet[]): MappingValidation
validateColumnMapping.description: Comprehensive validation of a column mapping covering type compatibility, size, nullable, precision, defaults, and constraints

checks.name: Type compatibility
checks.description: Uses checkTypeCompatibility() from typeMatrix. If 'incompatible' -> error. If warning exists and compatible -> warning.

checks.name: Size/Length
checks.description: Extracts type params from both. If source length > target length -> warning about truncation.

checks.name: Nullable
checks.description: Source nullable determined by YES/Y uppercase check. If source allows NULL but target is NOT NULL -> error (migration may fail).

checks.name: Precision/Scale
checks.description: For NUMBER/NUMERIC/DECIMAL types: if source precision > target precision -> warning. If source scale > target scale -> warning about decimal truncation.

checks.name: Default value
checks.description: If source has default but target does not -> warning.

checks.name: Constraint check
checks.description: If source column is PK but target is not -> warning. If source has UNIQUE but target has neither UNIQUE nor PK -> warning.

validateColumnMapping.returnType: MappingValidation = { typeMatch, sizeMatch, nullableMatch, precisionMatch, defaultMatch, constraintMatch, warnings: string[], errors: string[] }

getOverallCompatibility.signature: export function getOverallCompatibility(typeCompatibility: TypeCompatibility, validation: MappingValidation): TypeCompatibility
getOverallCompatibility.description: Determines overall compatibility from type check result and validation
logic: If typeCompatibility is 'needs_conversion' OR validation has warnings -> return 'needs_conversion'
If any of sizeMatch/precisionMatch/defaultMatch/constraintMatch is false -> return 'needs_conversion'

levenshteinDistance.signature: function levenshteinDistance(str1: string, str2: string): number
levenshteinDistance.description: Standard dynamic programming Levenshtein distance implementation. O(m*n) time and space.

findBestMatch.signature: export function findBestMatch(sourceCol: Column, targetCols: Column[], usedTargetCols: Set<string>, sourceIndex: number, totalSourceCols: number): MatchScore | null
findBestMatch.description: Finds the best matching target column for a source column using multi-factor scoring

scoringRules.description: Same base type, score based on relative position similarity
Added to existing score if base types match (capped at 1.0)

findBestMatch.returnType: MatchScore = { column: Column, score: number, matchType: 'exact' | 'case_insensitive' | 'similar' | 'type_position' } | null

autoMapColumns.signature: export function autoMapColumns(sourceTable: Table, targetTable: Table, sourceScriptId: string, targetScriptId: string, ruleSets: TypeRuleSet[]): ColumnMapping[]
autoMapColumns.description: Auto-maps columns between source and target tables. Iterates source columns, finds best match in target (excluding already-used target columns). Creates ColumnMapping with validation.

findMatchingTable.signature: export function findMatchingTable(sourceTable: Table, targetTables: Table[]): Table | null
findMatchingTable.description: Finds matching target table for source table

autoMapProject.signature: export function autoMapProject(sourceTables, targetTables, sourceScriptId, targetScriptId, sourceDb, targetDb, customRules?): { mappings: ColumnMapping[], tableMappings: TableMapping[] }
autoMapProject.description: Auto-maps all tables and their columns between source and target scripts

createManualMapping.signature: export function createManualMapping(sourceCol, targetCol, sourceTable, targetTable, sourceScriptId, targetScriptId, ruleSets): ColumnMapping
createManualMapping.description: Creates a manual column mapping with validation. mapType='manual', confidence=1.0

createTransformation.signature: export function createTransformation(type, sequence, params?, expression?): Transformation
createTransformation.description: Creates a Transformation object with generated ID

previewTransformation.signature: export function previewTransformation(sampleValue: string, transformations: Transformation[]): string
previewTransformation.description: Applies a chain of transformations to a sample value for preview

transformationTypes.substring: result.substring(start, start + length) - params: start (default 0), length (optional)
transformationTypes.default_value: If result is empty/null/NULL, use params.defaultValue
transformationTypes.concat: prefix + result + suffix - params: prefix, suffix

previewTransformation.sortOrder: Transformations sorted by sequence before applying

sanitizeSheetName.description: Replaces invalid Excel sheet name characters with underscore, truncates to 31 chars

deduplicateSheetNames.description: Appends _1, _2, etc. suffix to duplicate sheet names. Adjusts truncation to accommodate suffix within 31 char limit.

estimateRowHeight.signature: function estimateRowHeight(values: string[], colWidths: number[]): number
estimateRowHeight.description: Estimates row height based on content length and column widths
estimateRowHeight.formula: lines = ceil(val.length / (charWidth * 0.9)) + (newlineCount). height = max(DATA_ROW_HEIGHT, maxLines * LINE_HEIGHT). Capped at MAX_ROW_HEIGHT.

applyMergedCell.signature: function applyMergedCell(sheet, row, col1, col2, value, font, fill, alignment?)
applyMergedCell.description: Merges cells if col2 > col1, sets value/font/fill/alignment/borders. Default alignment: { vertical: 'middle', horizontal: 'left', wrapText: true }

exportDataDictionaryToExcel.signature: export async function exportDataDictionaryToExcel(options: ExcelExportOptions): Promise<void>
exportDataDictionaryToExcel.description: Generates and downloads a multi-sheet Excel workbook as a Data Dictionary

indexSheet.name: Index
sections: Table List group (non-source tables): numbered with schema, table name (hyperlink to sheet), description, column count
Source Tables group (isSource tables): same format
Appendix links: Master Code Definitions link, Master Code Categories link
features: Table Name cells are hyperlinks to respective table sheets
Table Name font: Consolas 10 bold, color 2563EB, underlined

tableSheets.nameFormat: prefix + tableName (prefix = 'SRC_' if isSource, else ''). Sanitized and deduplicated.

sections.name: Table Information
sections.format: Label in col 1 (bold, LABEL_BG), value merged across remaining columns

sections.name: Constraints
sections.note: Shows 'No constraints defined' in italic if empty

sections.name: Column Details
sections.dynamicColumns: Based on visibleColumns option - filters from EXCEL_COLUMNS

dataMapping.sampleValues: First 5 values joined by ', ', with '...' if more than 5
dataMapping.mappedTo: getMappingInfo result, or 'Not Mapped [- comment]' if migrationNeeded=false, or '-'
dataMapping.migrationNeeded: '' if undefined, 'Y' if true, 'N' if false

sections.autoFilter: Applied on column header row spanning all data rows

appendixSheet.name: Appendix
appendixSheet.condition: Only created if masterCodes array is non-empty

appendixCategoriesSheet.name: Appendix - Categories
appendixCategoriesSheet.condition: Only created if masterCodeCategories array is non-empty

downloadMechanism: 3. Create object URL, create <a> element, set href and download='{scriptName}.xlsx'
4. Append to body, click, remove, revoke URL

getDefaultDatatypeMappings.signature: export function getDefaultDatatypeMappings(sourceType: string, targetType: string): DatatypeMapping[]
getDefaultDatatypeMappings.description: Returns default datatype mappings based on source-target key. Keys: 'oracle-postgresql' and 'postgresql-oracle'. Others return empty array.

adjustDatatypeSize.signature: export function adjustDatatypeSize(currentType: string, referenceType: string): string
adjustDatatypeSize.description: Keeps existing base type, adjusts size/precision from reference type. If reference has size, applies it to current base type. Otherwise returns current base type.

generateAlignmentSql.signature: export function generateAlignmentSql(mappings, sourceScript, targetScript, direction, includeNullable, includeDatatype, datatypeMappings): string
generateAlignmentSql.description: Generates ALTER TABLE SQL statements to align schemas between source and target

postgresql: ALTER TABLE {schema}.{table} ALTER COLUMN {col} DROP NOT NULL;
ALTER TABLE {schema}.{table} ALTER COLUMN {col} SET NOT NULL;

generateAlignmentSql.headerFormat: SQL comment block with direction, modify/reference scripts, generation timestamp, and options

extractSize.description: Extracts content inside parentheses from a type string

myersDiff.signature: function myersDiff(oldLines: string[], newLines: string[]): DiffEdit[]
myersDiff.description: Implementation of Myers' diff algorithm for computing the shortest edit script between two arrays of strings
algorithm: 1. Forward pass: iterate d from 0 to max(n+m)
- For each k from -d to d (step 2):
- Determine x from v[k-1]+1 (move right/delete) or v[k+1] (move down/insert)
- Follow diagonal while lines match (equal lines)
- If x >= n and y >= m: found shortest path, backtrack
- Save v snapshot in trace array
2. Backtrack through trace to construct DiffEdit operations (equal, insert, delete)

computeVersionDiff.signature: export function computeVersionDiff(oldVersion: ScriptVersion, newVersion: ScriptVersion, contextLines?: number, includeSchema?: boolean, showAllLines?: boolean): VersionCompareResult
computeVersionDiff.description: Main API for computing diff between two script versions
algorithm: 2. Compute Myers diff to get edit operations
4. Group into hunks OR create single hunk (if showAllLines)
5. If includeSchema and both have .data: compute schema diff with compareTables()

generateDiffMarkers.signature: export function generateDiffMarkers(hunks: DiffHunk[], totalLines: number): DiffMarker[]
generateDiffMarkers.description: Generates minimap markers from hunks. Maps hunk positions to percentages of total lines.

getAllLineDiffs.description: Flattens all hunks into a single array of LineDiffs

findNextChange.signature: export function findNextChange(hunks: DiffHunk[], currentLine: number): number | null
findNextChange.description: Finds start line of next change hunk after currentLine

findPrevChange.signature: export function findPrevChange(hunks: DiffHunk[], currentLine: number): number | null
findPrevChange.description: Finds start line of previous change hunk before currentLine

computeCharDiff.signature: export function computeCharDiff(oldStr: string, newStr: string): { oldChars: CharDiff[], newChars: CharDiff[] }
computeCharDiff.description: Character-level diff using common prefix/suffix approach
algorithm: 1. Handle edge cases: both empty, only old, only new
2. Find common prefix length (character-by-character from start)
3. Find common suffix length (character-by-character from end, not overlapping prefix)
4. Split into: prefix (unchanged) + middle (old=deleted, new=added) + suffix (unchanged)

buildUnifiedRows.description: Builds side-by-side unified diff rows for GitHub-style table display
buildUnifiedRows.pairingLogic: When a 'deleted' line is immediately followed by an 'added' line, they are paired as a 'modified' row with character-level diff highlights
buildUnifiedRows.type: UnifiedDiffRow = { oldLineNum, newLineNum, oldContent, newContent, type, oldChars?, newChars? }

getChangeIndex.signature: export function getChangeIndex(hunks: DiffHunk[], lineNumber: number): number
getChangeIndex.description: Returns 0-based index of the change hunk containing lineNumber, or -1

groupIntoHunks.signature: function groupIntoHunks(lineDiffs: LineDiff[], contextLines?: number): DiffHunk[]
groupIntoHunks.description: Groups line diffs into hunks with context lines. Closes a hunk when unchanged lines exceed contextLines*2. Adds leading/trailing context of contextLines.

hunkTypes.modification: Has modifications, or has both additions and deletions

types.ParsedCSVTable: { tableName: string; headers: string[]; rows: ParsedCSVRow[] }

parseCSVLine.description: Parses a CSV line handling quoted fields with commas. Escaped quotes are double-quote pairs (""). Fields are trimmed.
algorithm: 2. In quotes: '""' -> escaped quote, '"' alone -> end quotes
3. Not in quotes: '"' -> start quotes, ',' -> field separator

isHeaderRow.description: Detects header rows by checking for table name columns or if 70%+ of values match /^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*$/ pattern

findTableNameColumnIndex.description: Finds index of table name column by checking against tableNamePatterns. Returns -1 if not found.

isMetadataLine.description: Detects metadata/garbage lines that should be skipped

rules: Not Java keywords (throws, try, catch, class, etc.)

findHeaderRowIndex.description: Scans first 10 lines for a valid header row (3+ columns, 50%+ match /^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*$/i). Returns 0 as default.

parseCSVSampleData.signature: export function parseCSVSampleData(content: string, fileName: string): ParsedCSVData
parseCSVSampleData.description: Parses CSV content into table-grouped data. Handles multi-table CSVs where TBL_NAME column identifies the table.
algorithm: 1. Split by newlines, filter empty lines
2. Find header row index, skip metadata
3. Parse header row, find TBL_NAME column index
a. Check if it's a new header row (table boundary)
b. Determine table name from TBL_NAME column or filename
d. Create row object (excluding TBL_NAME column)

stripTablePrefix.description: Strips common table prefixes from column names
patterns: Pattern 3: Same regex applied again (handles BE_MNETD_ double prefix)

findMatchingColumn.signature: function findMatchingColumn(csvColumn: string, scriptColumns: string[]): string | null
matchOrder: 2. Stripped CSV vs original script column
3. Original CSV vs stripped script column
4. Stripped CSV vs stripped script column (min length 3)
5. Suffix matching: one ends with the other (min length 4)

extractCoreTableName.description: Extracts core table identifier by removing common prefixes and suffixes

matchOrder: 2. Core name match (extracted cores compared, min length 3)
3. Core containment (one core contains the other, min length 4)
5. Partial match (one contains the other, min length 5)
6. FALLBACK: Column-based matching (findBestMatchByColumns with min 30% score and 3+ column matches)
findMatchingTable.returnType: { table: Table | null; matchMethod: 'name' | 'columns'; matchScore?: number }

matchSampleDataToScript.signature: export function matchSampleDataToScript(csvData: ParsedCSVData, script: Script): SampleDataMatchResult[]
matchSampleDataToScript.description: Matches CSV data to script tables and columns. Returns match results with matched/unmatched columns.

extractSampleValues.signature: export function extractSampleValues(csvData, matchResults, maxValues?): Map<string, Map<string, string[]>>
extractSampleValues.description: Extracts first N unique non-empty values for each matched column. Returns nested Map: tableName -> columnName -> values[].

applySampleValuesToScript.signature: export function applySampleValuesToScript(script: Script, sampleValues: Map<string, Map<string, string[]>>): Script
applySampleValuesToScript.description: Returns new Script with sampleValues applied to matching columns. Updates both targets and sources.

removeSampleValuesFromScript.description: Removes sampleValues property from all columns using destructuring rest operator.

createSampleDataAttachment.signature: export function createSampleDataAttachment(fileName, matchResults, parseErrors): SampleDataAttachment
createSampleDataAttachment.description: Creates attachment record with ID, fileName, uploadedAt, matchResults, and warnings (including unmatched tables/columns).

attachSampleDataToScript.signature: export function attachSampleDataToScript(script, csvContent, fileName): { script: Script; attachment: SampleDataAttachment }
attachSampleDataToScript.description: Full pipeline: parseCSV -> match -> extract -> apply -> create attachment -> append to script.sampleDataAttachments

removeSampleDataAttachment.description: Removes an attachment by ID. If no attachments remain, clears all sample values from script.

configuration.isLoggingGuard: Boolean flag prevents infinite loops when logger itself logs

LogEntry.message: string (args joined with space, objects JSON.stringify'd)

initDebugLogger.description: Replaces console.log/warn/error/info with intercepting wrappers. Original methods are stored and still called. Uses isLogging guard to prevent recursion.

getLogs.description: Returns a shallow copy of the logs array

clearLogs.description: Empties the logs array and notifies listeners

subscribeToLogs.description: Subscribes to log changes. Returns unsubscribe function.

formatTimestamp.description: Formats timestamp to HH:MM:SS.mmm using toLocaleTimeString with 24-hour format and fractionalSecondDigits: 3

exportLogsAsText.description: Formats all logs as text: '[HH:MM:SS.mmm] LEVEL message'. Level is uppercased and padded to 5 chars.

downloadLogs.description: Downloads logs as text file named 'debug-logs-YYYY-MM-DD.txt' via Blob/URL/anchor pattern

notificationMechanism.description: Uses debounced setTimeout(16ms) + requestAnimationFrame to defer notifications outside React's synchronous render cycle

localStorageKeys.erd_positions_{scriptId}: Per-script ERD positions (legacy, used in exportWorkspace)

types.GitSyncSettings: { path: string | null; enabled: boolean; lastSaved: number | null }

getGitSyncSettings.description: Reads path, enabled, lastSaved from localStorage

setGitSyncPath.signature: export function setGitSyncPath(path: string | null): void
setGitSyncPath.description: Sets or removes git sync path in localStorage

triggerGitSync.description: Immediate manual sync. Cancels any pending debounced sync. Temporarily enables sync if disabled.

gitSync.mechanism: performGitSync() exports workspace, calls electronAPI.saveWorkspaceToPath(fullPath, workspace)

loadScripts.description: JSON.parse from localStorage, returns [] on error

getSortedScripts.description: Sorts scripts by stored order. Scripts not in order list are placed at end.

reparseScript.description: Re-parses a script by ID. Loads scripts, re-parses rawContent, updates, saves, dispatches storage event.

exportWorkspace.description: Collects all data from localStorage into a WorkspaceData object

importWorkspace.description: Restores all workspace data to localStorage. Saves scripts, flowcharts (if present), mapping projects, type rule sets, theme, theme variant, ERD positions, DD settings. Triggers scheduleElectronSave().

getWorkspaceSummary.signature: export function getWorkspaceSummary(): { scripts, flowcharts, mappingProjects, typeRuleSets, totalSize }
getWorkspaceSummary.description: Returns counts and total JSON size in MB

loadWorkspaceFromElectron.description: Loads workspace from Electron file system on startup via loadWorkspaceFromFile()

downloadJson.signature: export function downloadJson(data: unknown, filename: string): void
downloadJson.description: Downloads data as JSON file via Blob/URL/anchor pattern

downloadText.signature: export function downloadText(content: string, filename: string): void

downloadYaml.signature: export function downloadYaml(content: string, filename: string): void
downloadYaml.description: Downloads content as YAML file (MIME: text/yaml)

loadMappingProjects.description: Loads from localStorage. Performs migration: adds missing validation property to old mappings with default values (all true, empty arrays).

loadMappingProject.signature: export function loadMappingProject(projectId: string): MappingProject | null

saveMappingProject.description: Upsert: updates existing by ID or appends new

saveTypeRuleSet.description: Upsert: updates existing by ID or appends new

migrateProjectTypeRules.description: Migrates inline project typeRules to global shared 'Default Type Rules' TypeRuleSet. Creates default if not exists. Merges rules avoiding duplicates (by sourcePattern + targetPattern). Assigns all projects to the shared default.

consolidateTypeRuleSets.description: Merges all TypeRuleSets into a single 'Default Type Rules'. Removes duplicates. Updates all projects. Skips if <= 1 exists.

getOrCreateDefaultTypeRuleSet.description: Returns existing 'Default Type Rules' or creates new one with default structure

exportRulesToYaml.description: Exports type rule set to YAML-like format with comments header

exportMappingProject.signature: export function exportMappingProject(project, sourceScript, targetScript): MappingExport
exportMappingProject.description: Creates structured export object for a mapping project

exportMappingToYaml.description: Converts MappingExport to YAML-like text format

migrateScriptToVersioning.description: Creates initial version from current state if no versions exist. Sets versioningEnabled=true, maxVersions=50.

createScriptVersion.signature: export function createScriptVersion(script: Script, message?: string): Script
createScriptVersion.description: Creates new version from current rawContent/data. Prepends to versions array (newest first). Enforces maxVersions limit by slicing.

setCurrentVersion.signature: export function setCurrentVersion(script: Script, versionId: string): Script
setCurrentVersion.description: Switches current version pointer. Updates rawContent and data from target version. Does NOT create new version.

restoreScriptVersion.signature: export function restoreScriptVersion(script: Script, versionId: string): Script

getScriptVersion.signature: export function getScriptVersion(script, versionId): ScriptVersion | null

getCurrentVersion.signature: export function getCurrentVersion(script: Script): ScriptVersion | null

getPreviousVersion.signature: export function getPreviousVersion(script: Script): ScriptVersion | null

hasUnsavedChanges.description: Compares rawContent to current version's content

deleteScriptVersion.description: Cannot delete the only version (throws). If deleting current, sets current to newest remaining.

setMaxVersions.signature: export function setMaxVersions(script: Script, maxVersions: number): Script
setMaxVersions.description: Clamps to 1-100. Trims versions if exceeding new limit.

getVersionsStorageSize.description: Estimates bytes: content.length * 2 (UTF-16) + JSON.stringify(data).length + 200 metadata overhead per version

ddColumnVisibility.backwardCompatibility: Old format was string[] (treated as both visible and knownColumns)

functions.loadDDVisibleColumns: Load per-script, handles legacy string[] format
functions.loadExcelExportColumns: Load saved Excel export column selection

formatStorageSize.description: Formats bytes as 'N B', 'N.N KB', or 'N.N MB'

MappingExport.project: { name, source: { scriptId, scriptName, type }, target: { scriptId, scriptName, type } }
MappingExport.columnMappings: Array<{ source: { table, column, type }, target: { table, column, type }, compatibility, transformations, remarks?, validation: { warnings, errors } }>

ElectronAPI.description: Interface for window.electronAPI bridge object exposed by Electron preload script

legacyAPIs.exportWorkspace: (data, filename?) => Promise<{ success, path?, canceled?, error? }>

saveWorkspaceToFile.signature: export async function saveWorkspaceToFile(data: WorkspaceData): Promise<boolean>
saveWorkspaceToFile.description: Saves workspace via electronAPI.saveWorkspace(). Returns false if not in Electron.

loadWorkspaceFromFile.signature: export async function loadWorkspaceFromFile(): Promise<WorkspaceData | null>
loadWorkspaceFromFile.description: Loads workspace via electronAPI.loadWorkspace(). Returns null if not in Electron.

exportWorkspaceWithPicker.signature: export async function exportWorkspaceWithPicker(data: WorkspaceData, filename?: string): Promise<boolean>

importWorkspaceWithPicker.signature: export async function importWorkspaceWithPicker(): Promise<WorkspaceData | null>

enableAutoSave.signature: export async function enableAutoSave(intervalMinutes?: number): Promise<boolean>

restoreBackup.signature: export async function restoreBackup(filename: string): Promise<WorkspaceData | null>

deleteBackup.signature: export async function deleteBackup(filename: string): Promise<boolean>

getDataDirectory.signature: export async function getDataDirectory(): Promise<string | null>

setupAutoSaveListener.description: Sets up listeners for auto-save complete and error events. Returns cleanup function.

electronStorage.errorHandling: All functions wrap Electron API calls in try/catch. Log success/failure. Return false/null on error. Guard against non-Electron environment.

methods.initialize: Initializes Google Drive if configured, starts auto-backups if enabled
methods.getCurrentData: Loads scripts and mapping projects from storage, returns BackupData { version: '1.0', exportDate, scripts, mappingProjects }
methods.updateSettings: Deep merge settings, save to localStorage, restart auto-backups
methods.openBackupDirectory: Opens backup directory in file explorer
methods.configureGoogleDrive: Saves config and initializes Google Drive
methods.restoreFromBackup: Restores by writing to localStorage keys 'dm_tool_data' and 'dm_tool_mapping_projects'
methods.restoreFromLatestLocal: Gets latest local backup and restores
methods.restoreFromLatestGoogleDrive: Gets latest Google Drive backup and restores
methods.getBackupStatus: Returns combined status of local and Google Drive backups

LocalBackupManager.filenameFormat: renaissance-backup-{ISO timestamp with colons/dots replaced by dashes, first 19 chars}.json

startAutoBackup.description: Creates initial backup immediately, then schedules periodic backups via setInterval

createBackup.description: Gets current data, generates timestamped filename, writes JSON with 2-space indent, cleans old backups (keep 20)

listBackups.description: Lists files matching 'renaissance-backup-*.json' in backup dir, sorted by mtime descending (newest first)

methods.cleanOldBackups: Deletes backups beyond keepCount (from sorted list)

exportToFile.description: Shows Electron save dialog with default filename 'renaissance-export-{timestamp}.json', filter for JSON files

importFromFile.description: Shows Electron open dialog for JSON files, reads and parses selected file

types.DriveFile: { id: string; name: string; createdTime: string; size: string }
types.GoogleDriveConfig: { clientId: string; clientSecret: string; redirectUri: string }

initialize.description: Sets config, loads saved access token from localStorage

mechanism: 1. Build auth URL with clientId, redirectUri, response_type=token, scope
6. On success: save token to localStorage and this.accessToken

logout.description: Clears accessToken, folderId, removes from localStorage, stops auto-sync

ensureBackupFolder.description: Finds or creates 'Renaissance DM Backups' folder in Google Drive
ensureBackupFolder.caching: Saves folderId to localStorage and instance

uploadBackup.description: Multipart upload to Google Drive API

listBackups.description: Lists files matching 'renaissance-backup-' in backup folder, ordered by createdTime desc

cleanOldBackups.description: Lists backups, deletes those beyond keepCount

startAutoSync.description: Performs initial sync, then schedules periodic sync via setInterval

syncNow.description: Gets data from callback, uploads, cleans old (keep 10)
